title: beikeshop目前最大的问题
author: createctoco
body:
**数据库几乎无管理：无批量清理**

1. 每笔订单触发 4-6 条通知（客户+管理员各1条，状态变更再加2条），每条的 data 字段存储了完整的 Order 模型序列化。一个日订单100的站点，一个月就产生 12,000-18,000 条通知
2. 还有退款记录，支付记录，各种记录永远不删，也不分表，使用时间越久，数据库冗余越多，最后导致网站越来越慢。
建议增加一个数据库管理插件，直接可以交给用户决定是否删除冗余数据，也可以定时自动处理
3.数据结构简单：表设计索引不够，订单多了会变慢

**支付不完整：支付宝插件只有支付、无原路退款，对账难**

1 订单退款，需要手动退款，点击同意退货之后，应该这样：卖家应该点击确认收到退货，同意退款，自动把款退回。现在官方的支付宝插件是个半成品，只收款不退款，更不能对账。

title: IDOR vulnerabilities in customer account controllers (Address, RMA)
author: lighthousekeeper1212
body:
## Summary

Multiple customer-facing account controllers contain **Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)** vulnerabilities where resource lookups use `find($id)` without verifying customer ownership, allowing any authenticated customer to access or delete other customers' data.

## Affected Files

### 1. AddressController (`beike/Shop/Http/Controllers/Account/AddressController.php`)

- **`show()` (line 37)**: Uses `AddressRepo::find($id)` without customer ownership check
- **`destroy()` (line 65)**: Uses `AddressRepo::delete($id)` without customer ownership check

**Compare with properly scoped methods:**
- `index()` (line 26) correctly uses `AddressRepo::listByCustomer(current_customer())`
- `AddressService::update()` (line 38) correctly checks `$address->customer_id != $customer->id`

### 2. RmaController (`beike/Shop/Http/Controllers/Account/RmaController.php`)

- **`show()` (line 45)**: Uses `RmaRepo::find($id)` without customer verification
- **`create()` (line 57)**: Uses `OrderProductRepo::find($orderProductId)` without customer verification

**Compare with properly scoped method:**
- `index()` (line 30) correctly uses `RmaRepo::listByCustomer(current_customer())`

## Impact

Any authenticated customer can:
- View other customers' addresses (PII: name, phone, full address)
- Delete other customers' addresses
- View other customers' RMA/return requests
- Create RMA requests against other customers' order products

## Suggested Fix

Add customer ownership verification in `AddressRepo::find()`, `AddressRepo::delete()`, and the RMA controller methods. For example:

```php
// In AddressController::show()
$address = AddressRepo::find($id);
if (!$address || $address->customer_id != current_customer()->id) {
    abort(404);
}
```

Or scope the queries to the current customer:

```php
$address = current_customer()->addresses()->findOrFail($id);
```

## Discovery

Found via manual code review comparing authorization patterns across controller methods (1-of-N inconsistency pattern).

title: Potential Issue Found: File Upload
author: bgeesaman
body:
I've been doing source code analysis of certain types of public repos for a specific classes of problems, and I found a something in your repo from my research that you may want to take a look at.

Specifically:
https://github.com/beikeshop/beikeshop/blob/1d6242993df95790948b57dd655666bb8a2be9e7/beike/Admin/Services/FileManagerService.php#L278


Laravel's file upload `storeAs()` function can be vulnerable to an arbitrary file upload flaw if the file name and path arguments are not properly validated and sanitized.  This means and attacker can potentially write files to places outside the intended location or even overwrite critical files on the server. Depending on permissions, this may provide a path for remote code execution.

See: https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Laravel_Cheat_Sheet.html#unrestricted-file-uploads for more details on how to improve validation of file names and paths.


> Note: This research has taken some time to complete, so the commit I'm referencing is a few weeks old. You may have already fixed this issue in a later commit. If so, feel free to ignore/close.  Just wanted to give you a heads up as a courtesy in case you found it helpful.
